commands.review_summary:
Adds an `openclaw_version` role variable, uses it in release-mode `pnpm install`, skips installs for matching exact semver pins, and documents the new option.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. Source inspection shows current main always installs `openclaw@latest`, while the PR adds a new pinning path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This PR needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for shell-safe version handling, exact-version skip behavior, and real behavior proof; automation should not proceed while the external proof gate is missing.

target.title: feat(openclaw): support pinning release version via openclaw_version
target.body:
## What
Adds an `openclaw_version` variable so release-mode installs can pin an exact OpenClaw version instead of always tracking `@latest`.

## Why
Release mode always ran `pnpm install -g openclaw@latest`. Two consequences:
1. **Not reproducible** — every converge could silently pull a newer release.
2. **Not idempotent** — the install step ran on every run.

On top of that, **not every OpenClaw release is equally stable** — some versions regress. The community even tracks this per-version at https://isitstable.iclaw.digital/#/openclaw. Always installing `@latest` means a deploy can silently land on a freshly-published, not-yet-proven release, which is risky for production fleets. Being able to pin to a known-good version (and roll forward deliberately once it's confirmed stable) is safer than tracking the moving `latest` tag.

## How
- New `openclaw_version` var, **default `latest`** → existing behavior unchanged.
- Set it to an exact version (e.g. `2026.6.9`) → install runs once, then is **skipped** while that version is present. Dist-tags/ranges like `latest` keep refreshing every run as before.
- Renamed an internal `register: openclaw_version` to `openclaw_installed_version` to free the variable name (no external impact).

## Testing (Ubuntu 24.04, `tests/run-tests.sh` harness)
- `latest` path: convergence + verify + idempotency all pass (`changed=0`).
- Pin `2026.6.9`: run 1 installs that exact version (not `latest` `2026.6.10`); run 2 skips the install task (`changed=0`).
- `yamllint`, `ansible-lint` (production profile), and `--syntax-check` all clean.

Docs updated: `defaults/main.yml`, `README.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`.
target.author: dmoka
